essence: Operates and maintains equipment that reduces air pollution from industrial and commercial sources.

identity.title: Air Pollution Control Technician
identity.summary: Installs, operates, and maintains emissions‑control systems, monitors air pollutants, and supports compliance with environmental regulations.

salary.notes: Technicians specialising in industrial emissions, advanced filtration, or regulatory inspections can exceed £52k-£65k+.

qualifications: Training in environmental science, mechanical engineering, or pollution control
Knowledge of emissions‑control systems and air‑quality regulations
Ability to operate monitoring instruments and maintain filtration equipment
Understanding of industrial processes and pollution‑prevention techniques

lifestyle.description: Air pollution control technicians work for industrial facilities, environmental contractors, and regulatory agencies. They maintain emissions‑control systems, monitor pollutants, troubleshoot equipment, and support compliance with environmental laws. The role suits individuals who enjoy hands‑on technical work, environmental protection, and system maintenance.

habits: Monitors air pollutants and records data
Collects samples and assists with pollution investigations
